title: The Cubic Plus Association Equation of State (CPA-EoS): From Fundamentals to Industrial Applications
novelty_statement: A reproducible technical monograph that connects CPA equation-of-state theory, NeqSim implementation details, and industrial applications for water-hydrocarbon, gas processing, and CO2 systems.

research_questions.question: How can CPA-EoS be derived from thermodynamic fundamentals and association theory in a form useful for engineering calculations?
research_questions.hypothesis: A layered treatment from cubic equations of state through Wertheim association theory makes the CPA formulation accessible without losing mathematical rigor.
research_questions.method: Trace the derivation through Parts I and II of the book and cross-check notation against the nomenclature and chapter equations.
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: All core CPA symbols, residual Helmholtz terms, site fractions, and mixture rules are defined consistently across the manuscript.

research_questions.question: Which numerical implementation details are required for robust CPA calculations in NeqSim?
research_questions.hypothesis: The book can document the practical solver choices, parameter workflows, and stability checks needed for reproducible CPA calculations.
research_questions.method: Review the numerical implementation chapter and associated notebooks for flash, parameter, and solver examples.
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: The manuscript includes executable examples and implementation guidance for CPA setup, parameter use, and solver behavior.

research_questions.question: Where does CPA-EoS provide practical value for industrial systems?
research_questions.hypothesis: CPA adds the most value in associating systems such as water-hydrocarbon phase behavior, glycol dehydration, hydrate-related calculations, and CO2 systems with water.
research_questions.method: Use the application chapters and generated figure dossier to document the covered industrial cases.
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: The final book covers water-hydrocarbon, gas processing, and CO2 application chapters with reproducible figures and cited references.

key_figures: Gas processing and CO2 system figures

replication.note: This book currently commits generated evidence and figure dossiers. Root-level numerical claim replication will be added as chapter notebooks are promoted to explicit experiments.
